learning_objective: Implement a cheap-first cascade with appropriate escalation triggers and quantify the expected cost savings.
specification:
Type: workflow **sim-id:** cheap-first-cascade-escalation
**Library:** Mermaid
**Status:** Specified Purpose: Show the cheap-first cascade flow with escalation triggers visible at each gate, plus a cost annotation showing per-request expected cost as a function of escalation rate. Bloom Level: Apply (L3) Bloom Verb: implement Learning objective: Implement a cheap-first cascade with appropriate escalation triggers and quantify the expected cost savings. Visual style: Flowchart with three branches Steps: 1. User request → Send to cheap model (Haiku, \$0.001) 2. Run quality gate: - Format check (JSON valid?) - Confidence check (logprob delta > threshold?) - Length check (within expected range?) 3. Branch: - If all pass → Return cheap response (cost: \$0.001) - If any fail → Escalate to expensive model (Sonnet, \$0.005), return that response (cost: \$0.001 + \$0.005 = \$0.006) 4. Optional second escalation to most-expensive model (Opus) on second failure Annotations: - Per-stage cost - Per-stage probability (default: 80% cheap-pass, 18% sonnet-pass, 2% opus-pass) - Expected cost per request: 0.80 × \$0.001 + 0.18 × \$0.006 + 0.02 × \$0.026 = \$0.0024 - Compared to "always Opus" baseline (\$0.026): 91% reduction Interactive controls: - Slider: cheap-pass rate (50%–95%) - Slider: confidence threshold - Toggle: "Add Opus tier" (on/off) - Toggle: "Show expected cost calculation" Implementation: Mermaid flowchart with cost annotations, responsive layout
